opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The reliability of web-scheduled tasks is brittle; consider a circuit breaker pattern to handle rate limits and transient errors gracefully. Routing requests based on cost and latency, rather than just model capability, becomes crucial at scale. You’ll quickly find that maintaining a robust task queue and observability layer is more important than the scheduling logic itself.
actions.reasoning: My work routing LLM requests across providers and building agent orchestration pipelines gives me a perspective on the challenges of scheduling tasks based on external web resources.
